name: Parrot
summary: Scarlet macaws are huge, rainbow-colored parrots of tropical rainforests. They fly over the canopy in pairs, crack hard nuts with strong beaks, and are among the most famous parrots in the world.
metaTitle: Parrot Facts | Rainforest Habitat, Diet & Photos
metaDescription: Discover scarlet macaw parrot facts with photos, rainforest habitat, fruit diet, chicks, and flight. Learn where macaws live and why they are so colorful.
searchIntents: how big is a scarlet macaw

lifespan.captivity: Up to 75 years in human care

behaviors: tool use with beak and feet
funFacts: Scarlet macaws can live more than 50 years in the wild.
They use their feet like hands to hold food.
Macaw pairs often stay together for many years.
Their loud calls can be heard far across the rainforest.
Scarlet macaws are bright red, yellow, and blue.
They nest in holes high up in old trees.

faq.question: What is a parrot?
faq.answer: A parrot is a bird with a hooked beak and feet that can grip branches. The scarlet macaw is one of the largest and most colorful parrots.

faq.question: Where do macaws live?
faq.answer: Scarlet macaws live in tropical rainforests from southern Mexico through Central America and into the Amazon region of South America.

faq.question: What do parrots eat?
faq.answer: Scarlet macaws eat fruits, nuts, seeds, and flowers. Their strong beaks crack open hard nuts.

faq.question: How big is a scarlet macaw?
faq.answer: Scarlet macaws are about 32 to 36 inches long including their long tail feathers.

faq.question: Are scarlet macaws endangered?
faq.answer: Scarlet macaws are listed as Least Concern overall, but forest loss and illegal pet trade have reduced numbers in some regions.

faq.question: How long do macaws live?
faq.answer: Wild scarlet macaws may live 40 to 50 years. In human care they can live even longer.

faq.question: Can parrots talk?
faq.answer: Many parrots can copy sounds. Scarlet macaws are better known for loud natural calls than for mimicking human speech.
